source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Growth
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a software engineer to own the systems that power the entire customer journey: how practices find us, sign up, and go live.

Independent health practices are stretched thin and need better tools. You'll help them discover Lassie and get successful fast. Working closely with sales and product, you'll build infrastructure that makes deals close smoothly and new customers successful from day one.

This role is ideal for someone who loves experimenting, moves fast, and wants to see their work directly impact revenue.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Are excited about experimenting, being wrong, and thinking from first principles

 - Create tools and systems that multiply the impact of sales and engineering

 - Collaborate directly with sales to turn messy workflows into streamlined systems

 - Influence how we approach marketing, brand, and distribution

 - Work across Python, TypeScript, various databases, serverless environments, and Salesforce, even if some are new to you




SOME THINGS WE'VE BUILT

 - Purpose-built growth and onboarding infrastructure that shortens the demo-to-close cycle from weeks to hours

 - Data pipelines processing $1B+ in insurance claims

 - A sync engine backed by encrypted SQLite in the browser, enabling 100ms experiences http://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-is-built-for-speed for healthcare payments and patient ledgers




TECHNOLOGY WE USE

We invest heavily in productivity. Engineers should have the best tools for the job and the freedom to choose them.

 - TypeScript, Next.js, Tailwind

 - Python (Django), Postgres, Redis

 - Salesforce, Snowflake, Hightouch

 - Google Cloud Platform, Vercel

 - Linear, Slack, Notion




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Executive at Lassie, you'll help the doctors’ offices understand what becomes possible when AI agents take on real operational work. You know how to build a pipeline, run demos, diagnose customer pain (or identify goals), build trust with SMB owners and staffers, and close customers ready to run a more automated office.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own a full-cycle SMB sales motion (prospecting, demos, follow-ups, close) against a monthly quota

 - Represent the Lassie brand and unlock referrals by building relationships with doctors, business owners, and communities

 - Improve Lassie's GTM playbook: what messaging converts, how to handle objections, and what the ideal customer looks like

 - Bring market feedback to product, marketing, and leadership so our pitch and product keep getting sharper




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 1–5 years of experience selling to small businesses

 - You built your own pipeline before

 - You move faster than everyone around you without sacrificing quality

 - You know how to read the room, ask for the sale, and get a deal done

 - You build real relationships with buyers, not transactional ones

 - You call things as they are (a weak pipeline, an objection, and more) and help fix them




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

source_jobs.title: Founding Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As Founding Recruiter, you'll help build the team that builds Lassie. You'll own recruiting end to end: finding exceptional talent, creating an excellent candidate experience, partnering closely with hiring managers, and raising the bar of what great looks like.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build Lassie's talent acquisition engine from scratch: strategy, process, tools, metrics, reporting, and brand

 - Own end-to-end recruiting across software engineering, GTM, and G&A

 - Partner with the founders and early hiring managers to define role requirements (MOCs) and design interview plans

 - Do initial screens, and manage candidates through every stage from first touch to offer close

 - Build talent pipelines across multiple simultaneous searches

 - Create and refine recruiting analytics (funnel health, conversion rates, time-to-hire metrics, and more)

 - Represent Lassie externally through events, meetups, and outbound messaging to raise awareness among top talent

 - Deliver an exceptional candidate experience and make sure every touchpoint reflects Lassie's values and ambition




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience and deep expertise in sourcing, assessing, and closing top talent

 - You've mastered mapping talent-dense networks, sourcing, and maintaining long-term relationships with top talent

 - You've designed or built AI-native workflows for recruiting tasks, or have a strong instinct for where AI eliminates manual work without compromising the candidate experience or quality of decision-making




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As Lassie's Head of Marketing, you'll be responsible for turning customer love into compounding demand. You'll connect product, data, marketing, sales, and customer insight to find the repeatable, scalable channels and messages that help Lassie reach far more doctors' offices efficiently. Think of this role as Lassie's Matt Epstein https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-EbxmSN_jcE.

You'll be in strong company. You'll partner directly with Lassie's CEO, who scaled Robinhood's referral program as its first growth lead. On Lassie's cap table, you'll find leading growth operators like Brian Balfour, former VP of Growth at HubSpot and founder of Reforge (one of the most respected growth courses in the industry), or John Egan, an engineering leader with deep growth experience at companies like Anthropic and Pinterest.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the growth system across acquisition, activation, and retention

 - Use customer insight, data, and experimentation to find the channels and messages that scale

 - Turn what's working into repeatable growth loops




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 5+ years of experience finding and scaling growth loops

 - You've worked at an early-stage startup

 - You bring rigor of thought: not only did you get a growth loop to work, but you can also articulate what you did and replicate it in a different context

 - You already use AI tools daily to build and operate, and you're excited to figure out how AI reshapes distribution and growth strategy from the ground up




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

We're hiring an engineer to build the AI agents that run doctor's offices.

These are production systems handling real money and real patient data for thousands of practices. You'll work across the full stack: agent architectures, data pipelines, evals, and the infrastructure that makes it all reliable.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

SOME THINGS WE'VE BUILT

 - A sync engine backed by encrypted SQLite in the browser, enabling 100ms experiences http://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-is-built-for-speed for healthcare payments and patient ledgers

 - TypeScript, React, Tailwind

 - Linear, Slack, Notion

source_jobs.title: Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a design-minded engineer to build the control plane for powerful agents handling billing and medical records. Real patients and real practices depend on these systems. You'll craft interfaces that make autonomous agents feel trustworthy and effortless to supervise.

We take a consumer approach to healthcare software: approachable, elegant, and sweating the details. The product spans onboarding, human-in-the-loop review, the tools practice owners use to understand their business, and more.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Strive for great, not good, and iterate until you get there

 - Take pride in building software that feels fast and effortless

 - Look for leverage: using tools, automation, and process to multiply your impact

 - Have strong fundamentals in JavaScript and browser APIs

 - Believe healthcare software should be as delightful as consumer products




SOME THINGS WE'VE BUILT

 - A sync engine backed by encrypted SQLite in the browser, enabling 100ms experiences http://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-is-built-for-speed for healthcare payments and patient ledgers

 - Systems that eliminate thousands of hours of paperwork every month

 - Data pipelines processing $1B+ in insurance claims




TECHNOLOGY WE USE

We invest heavily in productivity. Engineers should have the best tools for the job and the freedom to choose them.

 - TypeScript, React, Tailwind

 - Python (Django, Pydantic AI), Postgres, Redis

 - Google Cloud Platform, Vercel

 - Linear, Slack, Notion




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. ABOUT THE ROLE

Business Operations builds the operating system behind Lassie’s growth. On enrollments, you’ll design and run the processes that move customers through setup, paperwork, system connections, and internal handoffs so Lassie can start doing useful work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own enrollment workflows across paperwork, system connections, status tracking, internal handoffs, and customer readiness

 - Build the processes, dashboards, and automation that make setup faster and less manual over time

 - Work across sales, customer operations, product, and engineering to remove operational bottlenecks

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As Lassie's first finance hire, you'll build out the company's financial operations from the ground up. You'll work alongside CEO Steijn Pelle and Lassie advisor Jason Warnick (former CFO, Robinhood). You'll own the transition from founder-led finance to a world-class, AI-native function. This is a unique opportunity to build the systems that shape how Lassie grows and operates.



This role is as strategic as it is operational. You'll be involved in every major financial decision, from daily tradeoffs to strategic finance projects, and you'll be the person who gives the leadership team confidence in where the company stands and where it's headed financially.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Establish and own the reporting of the company's key metrics (think active practices, ACV, ARR, net dollar retention, churn, payback period) so you can measure progress

 - Build and maintain the company's financial operating model, including scenario planning and tradeoff visibility, so leadership can make confident resource and growth decisions

 - Ensure an accurate, timely monthly close and deliver financial statements that give leadership an actionable view of the business

 - Own the full FP&A cycle: budget, quarterly actuals, monthly reporting

 - Serve as the CEO's key finance partner through future fundraises

 - Build scalable, AI-native finance infrastructure across accounting, forecasting, spend management, and payroll, choosing tools that reduce complexity and grow with the company without overbuilding

 - Lead strategic finance work beyond the core: pricing structure, sales commission design, rev ops foundations, and more




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR




MUST-HAVE

 - You've built operating models, scenario plans, and unit economics from scratch in a startup environment, and you can defend your assumptions with board-level rigor

 - You're a hands-on operator: you've closed the books, managed vendors, and run payroll, not just managed people who do those things

 - You translate complex financial data into clear insights for a non-finance audience and are comfortable presenting to founders, board members, and investors

 - You have a track record of implementing finance systems and building processes from scratch. You know when the current tool is right-sized and when it's time to graduate, and you don't over-engineer

 - You've designed or built AI-native workflows for finance tasks, or have a strong instinct for where AI eliminates manual work without adding fragility


NICE TO HAVE

 - You've owned a fundraising process or been the key finance partner in one. You know how to organize a data room, work through diligence questions, and build investor confidence through metrics and narrative

 - You've managed a transition from outsourced to in-house accounting

 - You've worked on rev ops, pricing strategy, or sales commission structures alongside core FP&A work




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.

source_jobs.title: GTM Associate
source_jobs.description:
Our mission is to help small businesses run themselves. We’re starting with doctors’ offices, building autonomous systems that handle busywork and complete the workflows that keep the office moving every day. Our team is small, flat, and ambitious. We value craft, intellectual rigor, and direct communication.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a GTM Associate at Lassie, you'll create the first spark of demand. You'll identify promising doctors' offices, run thoughtful outreach you'd want to receive yourself, schedule demos, and help us learn which messages actually land with small businesses.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build lists of promising doctors' offices and run thoughtful outreach across email, phone, social, events, and more

 - Experiment with messages, segments, and channels to learn where demand is strongest

 - Run early discovery conversations to understand who's working in the practice, how the office operates, and where Lassie may or may not fit

 - Partner closely with Account Executives to schedule demos with offices that meet Lassie's ideal customer profile

 - Keep the GTM engine organized, measured, and moving




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 1–2 years of SDR or BDR experience

 - You move fast, think fast, and improve with every rep

 - You never give up, with a smile on your face

 - You build real rapport with a busy doctor in 90 seconds, and they feel it

 - You're self-aware, flag what isn't working, and know how to improve

 - You're excited about helping a small business run itself




JOINING US

To start, you'll have a handful of conversations with our team and founders. If there is mutual excitement, we'll invite you to the office where you'll work on a small project and meet the team.
